candidates.title: Prepare approval packet for The Posh Paw outreach
candidates.objective: Turn The Posh Paw outreach into a complete approval packet while preserving every external-action gate.

source.name: Prepare approval packet for The Posh Paw outreach
source.title: Prepare approval packet for The Posh Paw outreach
source.description: Present the complete reviewed body in the approval summary and wait for explicit send approval.
source.operatorNote: Approve sending the existing reviewed Gmail draft to The Posh Paw. This task prepares the packet only; it must not send, publish, submit, commit, deploy, spend, or make commitments.
source.draftSubject: The Posh Paw: quick customer-journey polish review
source.draftBody:
Hi Jane,

I came across The Posh Paw while looking at local Northampton pet-care websites.
The site already has a lot going for it: strong local positioning, clear trust signals, City & Guilds/insurance messaging, and the training academy gives it more depth than a standard grooming site.

One thing I noticed is that there may be a useful quick win around customer journey polish. Because the site covers grooming, spa treatments, courses, model-dog opportunities and contact routes, a few small changes could make it easier for mobile visitors to choose the right next step.

The kind of review I mean would be practical and public-site only:

- making grooming, treatments and course routes easier to scan
- tightening how trust proof is presented
- making the call/contact path clearer for customers who are ready to enquire

I run Tail Wagging Websites Design Factory Northampton and I have been doing short local website reviews for pet-care businesses. If useful, I can send over a concise review of the public site with a few practical fixes, no obligation.

Either way, The Posh Paw looks like a strong local business with a lot of proof already in place.

Best,
John
Tail Wagging Websites Design Factory Northampton

candidates.approvalReason: Internal packet preparation is safe; the described external action still needs John's explicit approval.

verification.description: The content worker prepares an evidence-grounded operator packet without taking the external action.
expectedEvidence: full proposed draft body when available

acceptanceCriteria: Packet states the requested approval clearly.
Packet cites current pipeline evidence and stage.
Packet includes the full proposed draft body when one exists.
Packet preserves send, publish, commit, deploy, spend, and commitment gates.
candidates.risk: low: local packet preparation only; external action remains gated

opportunity.description: Gmail draft exists and is unsent; send remains explicitly approval-gated.

score.formula: expectedBusinessImpact * confidence * urgency * commercialReadinessImpact * evidenceQuality * reversibility / (effort + operationalRisk + dependencyLoad + approvalFriction)
